meta.setting: Trail blazes, axe cuts, cairns, bent nails, chalk smears, branded stakes, and false route marks across Jefferson back roads
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with navigation pressure, false signs, pursuit, local knowledge, and route-choice risk

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Road movement, mining, logging, ranching, and rugged local travel around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Gold-rush movement, commerce, and Northern California settlement texture.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Trail Blazes region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Axe Cut, Bent Nail, and Chalk Smear
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: trail-mark object, weather or tampering detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing trail blazes as navigation aid, warning system, evidence trail, false-mark trick, and local-knowledge scene
closing blockquote that gives a generated trail-blaze sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate trail blazes as practical 1899 marks: axe cuts in pine bark, cairns, bent nails, branded stakes, chalk arrows on rock, cut brush, and charcoal marks that weather away.
Give the table hooks around a false blaze, a mark hidden by snowmelt, a pursuit route, a logging cutoff, a miner's private path, or a warning meant only for people who know the road.
Make marks readable but uncertain; the page should help decide age, maker, direction, tampering, weather damage, and whether a shortcut is a trap.
Keep the marks local and workmanlike, never invented alphabets, sacred signs, puzzle symbols, or secret-society codes.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: A good trail mark should look plain until somebody notices it points wrong.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A trail-blaze scene should ask who cut the mark, who changed it, what weather hid it, and what road it wants people to take.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western trail, timber, road, and tracking language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/trail-blazes.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not use stone-marker framing, old-world alphabets, sacred-invented marks, secret codes, or puzzle-symbol logic
do not include modern trail blazes, bright plastic flagging, reflective markers, park signage, spray paint, GPS, or recreational hiking language
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about trail blazes as navigation, evidence, false signs, and scene engine.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
